### ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/163044506763.htm|SHA2: Form hash value using SHA2 (S7-1500)

_ItemId: ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/163044506763.htm|SHA2: Form hash value using SHA2 (S7-1500)_

SHA2: Form hash value using SHA2
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Hash function and hash value
 A hash function is a mathematical function. It converts data of any length into strings of fixed length. The result of this conversion is called a hash value. The characters that are allowed for the hash value are determined via the hash function.
 A hash function has the following properties:
 
 
 Uniqueness: Identical data has the same hash value. 
 
 
 Irreversibility: It must not be possible to recover the original data from the calculated hash value.
 
 
 Collision-free: Different data also has different hash values.
 
 
 One can therefore verify the identity of two data areas by comparing their hash values. This makes it possible, for example, to determine whether data has been falsified during a data transmission.
 
 Definition: SHA
 The abbreviation SHA stands for Secure Hash Algorithm. The secure hash algorithm refers to a group of standardized hash functions.
 SHA1 was a very important cryptographic hash function in the 1990s. It has been considered insecure since 2004.
 SHA2 differs from SHA1 in several features and functions. SHA2 includes SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384 and SHA-512. The num


|  | Maximum number of "simultaneously" active SHA2 instructions |
| --- | --- |
| S7-1500-CPU | 1 |


| Parameters | Declaration | Data type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| REQ | Input | BOOL | Control parameter Request Activates the job on a rising edge |
| Data | Input | VARIANT | Data from which the hash value is to be calculated All data types except "BOOL" and "Array of BOOL" are permitted. When transferring the data from or to another computer system, you need to ensure that the data type there is the same as on the PLC. |
| DataLen | Input | UDINT | Length of the data area from which the hash value is to be calculated, in bytesIf you specify 0 here, the length of the tag pointed to by the "Data" parameter is used. |
| HashLen | Input | UINT | Length of the hash value to be calculated in bits The following values are supported: 224, 256, 384 and 512 |
| Done | Output | BOOL | Status parameter: 0: Job not yet started or still being processed. 1: Job completed without error. This state is only displayed for one call. |
| Busy | Output | BOOL | Status parameter: 0: Job not yet started or already completed. 1: Job not yet completed. A new job with this instance cannot be started. |
| Error | Output | BOOL | Status parameter: 0: No error occurred. 1: Error occurred during processing. The "Status" parameter supplies detailed information on the type of error. This state is only displayed for one call. |
| Status | Output | WORD | Error information |
| Hash | InOut | VARIANT | Hash value Permitted data type: Array of BYTE |


| STATUS (W#16#...) | Explanation |
| --- | --- |
| 0000 | "SHA2" was completed without errors. |
| 7000 | No job processing active |
| 7001 | Start of job execution |
| 7002 | Intermediate call (REQ irrelevant): |
| 8080 | "DataLen" does not match the length of the area pointed to by the "Data" parameter. |
| 8082 | "HashLen" does not match the length of the area to which the "Hash" parameter points. |
| 8083 | The length in VARIANT of the "Data" parameter was changed during processing, e.g. by a download. Note: You must ensure that the content of the "Data" parameter is not changed while "SHA2" is being processed. "SHA2" namely does not monitor whether the content of "Data" is changed; such monitoring would significantly reduce the performance of the PLC. If the content of "Data" is changed, "SHA2" is still executed, but the calculated hash value is not meaningful. |
| 80A3 | The value of the "HashLen" parameter is not supported. |
| 80C3 | Insufficient resources or temporary memory bottleneck |
| 80E5 | Internal error |
| 8224 | The "Data" parameter points to an impermissible area, for example to the load memory or the local data. |
| 8924 | The "Hash" parameter points to an impermissible area, for example to the load memory or the local data. |
| 8251 | The "BOOL" and "Array of BOOL" data types are not permitted for the "Data" parameter. |
| 8951 | Incorrect data type for the "Hash" parameter. Only the "Array of BYTE" data type is permitted. |
| 8252 | The variable at the "DATA" parameter is NULL or cannot be accessed because, for example, the DB is too small or does not exist. |
| 8952 | The variable at the "Hash" parameter is NULL or cannot be accessed because, for example, the DB is too small or does not exist. |
| xxyy, xx > 80 | For general error information, refer to Evaluating errors with output parameter RET_VAL |


### ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/108238007179.htm|RH_CTRL: Influencing sequences in R/H systems (S7-1500)

_ItemId: ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/108238007179.htm|RH_CTRL: Influencing sequences in R/H systems (S7-1500)_

RH_CTRL: Influencing sequences in R/H systems
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Description
 With the instruction "RH_CTRL", you can influence R/H systems as follows:
 
 
 You can disable the system state "SYNCUP". The lock is in force until you revoke it with the "RH_CTRL" instruction or until the R/H system goes into "STOP" mode.
 
 
 You can re-enable the system state "SYNCUP".
 
 
 You can request the system state "SYNCUP".
 
 
 You can request that the primary CPU goes into "STOP" mode.
 
 
 You can request that the backup CPU goes into "STOP" mode.
 
 
 You can query whether the "SYNCUP" system state is currently locked.
 
 
 You can request that one of the two R/H-CPUs goes into the "STOP" mode and takes on the role of the backup CPU.
 This is required if the R/H system is in a non-defined state, for example when both redundancy connections were interrupted at the same time and both R/H-CPUs have taken on the role of the primary CPU.
 
 
 You can specify that the "SYNCUP" system state be run through without the intended MRP check when there is at least one MRP ring present. (MRP check in this context means the following: A check is carried out to determine whether the MRP ring in which


|  | Danger |
| --- | --- |
| Prevent bodily injury and property damage Note that running through the "SYNCUP" system state without an MRP check can: Endanger human life or health Cause errors in IO devices, dangerous operating states and damage to the entire system. It is therefore strongly recommended that the transition to the "SYNCUP" system state without a prior MRP check be carried out only in commissioning scenarios and not during productive operation of your system. |


| Parameter | Declaration | Data type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| REQ | Input | BOOL | Control parameter Request Enables the execution of the instruction when REQ = "1". |
| Mode | Input | BYTE | Using the "Mode" parameter, select how you want to influence the R/H system: 3: Disable execution of the SYNCUP (as of firmware version V2.6 of the S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 4: Re-enable execution of the SYNCUP (as of firmware version V2.6 of the S7-1500-R/H CPUs) If applicable, switch on the intended MRP check in "SYNCUP" system state again (as of firmware version V3.1 of the S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 7: Request SYNCUP system state (as of firmware version V2.9 of S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 8: Request STOP of the primary CPU (as of firmware version V2.9 of the S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 9: Request STOP of the backup CPU (as of firmware version V2.9 of S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 10: Query whether the system state SYNCUP is currently locked (from firmware version V3.0 of the S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 11: Request STOP of an R/H-CPU and assign the role of the backup CPU to it (as of firmware version V3.1 of the S7-1500 R/H-CPUs) Requirement: The corresponding R/H-CPU is in "RUN" mode and there is no pairing. 14: Switch off the intended MRP check in "SYNCUP" system state (as of firmware version V3.1 of the S7-1500-R/H CPUs) 15: Enable a configured system IP address on the PROFINET interface specified at the "Submode" parameter. The activation status applies only to the "RUN-Redundant" system state. (as of firmware version V4.0 of S7-1500-R/H CPUs)Requirement: "RUN-Redundant" system state 16: Determine the activation status of the system IP address on the PROFINET interface specified at the "Submode" parameter for the "RUN-Redundant" system state (as of firmware version V4.0 of S7-1500-R/H CPUs) Requirement: "RUN-Redundant" system state |
| Submode | Input | WORD | With Mode = 3 to Mode = 14: Hidden parameter. It must have the value 0. With Mode = 15: Hardware identifier of the PROFINET interface (Xn or W1) on which the system IP address is to be enabled for the "RUN-Redundant" system state. With Mode = 16: Hardware identifier of the PROFINET interface (Xn or W1) on which the activation status of the system IP address is to be determined in the "RUN-Redundant" system state. Note for Mode = 15 and Mode = 16: The "Submode" parameter has data type WORD, while the hardware identifier has the data type UINT. Before calling "RH_CTRL", you need to convert the data type of the hardware identifier to WORD: with the "MOVE" instruction in LAD and "UINT_TO_WORD" instruction in SCL |
| Ret_Val | Output | INT | Error code |
| Busy | Output | BOOL | Status parameter 0: Execution of the instruction complete. 1: Execution of the instruction not yet complete. |


| Error code* (W#16#...) | Explanation | Relevant for mode |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | No error. | All |
| 1 | With Mode = 3: The "SYNCUP" system state is already disabled. With Mode = 4: The "SYNCUP" system state is already enabled. With Mode=14: The intended MRP check in "SYNCUP" system state is already switched off. With Mode=15: The system IP address has already been enabled on the specified PROFINET interface for the "RUN-Redundant" system state. | 3, 4, 14, 15 |
| 3 | The "SYNCUP" system state was disabled by "RH_CTRL" at an earlier time and is still disabled. | 10 |
| 4 | Currently there is no disable of the "SYNCUP" system state caused by "RH_CTRL". The intended MRP check in "SYNCUP" system state is switched on. | 10 |
| 5 | The system IP address is enabled on the specified PROFINET interface for the "RUN-Redundant" system state. | 16 |
| 6 | The system IP address is disabled on the specified PROFINET interface for the "RUN-Redundant" system state. | 16 |
| 7 | A switchover is in progress for the system IP address belonging to the specified PROFINET interface. | 16 |
| 14 | Currently there is no disable of the "SYNCUP" system state caused by "RH_CTRL". An MRP check does not take place in "SYNCUP" system state. | 10 |
| 7000 | No job in progress (REQ = 0). | All |
| 7001 | REQ = 1 at first call: Data transfer triggered; BUSY has the value "1". | All |
| 7002 | Intermediate call (REQ irrelevant): Data transfer already active; BUSY has the value 1. | All |
| 8082 | Disabling the "SYNCUP" system state is no longer possible, because the R/H system is already in this system state. | 3 |
| 8090 | Invalid value of the "Mode" parameter. | - |
| 8091 | Invalid value of the "Submode" parameter. | All: 15, 16: Invalid value All others: Value not 0 |
| 80A0 | The "SYNCUP" system state cannot be requested: The R/H system is not in the "RUN-Solo" system state. The R/H system is in the "RUN-Solo" system state, but there is no successful pairing between the CPUs. | 7 |
| 80A1 | The R/H system is not in "RUN-Redundant" system state. | 8, 9, 15, 16 |
| 80A2 | The following requirement is not fulfilled: The R/H-CPU is in "RUN" mode and there is no pairing. | 11 |
| 80A3 | A system IP address has not been configured for the specified PROFINET interface. | 15, 16 |
| 80A4 | A switchover of the system IP address is already in progress for the specified PROFINET interface. The current call of "RH_CTRL" with Mode = 15 has no effect. | 15 |
| 80A5 | The system IP address cannot be switched when OPC UA server is running. | 15 |
| * The error codes in the program editor are displayed as integer or hexadecimal values. For information on switching the display formats, refer to "See also". |


### ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/108238718603.htm|RH_GetPrimaryID: Determining the redundancy ID of the primary CPU (S7-1500)

_ItemId: ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/108238718603.htm|RH_GetPrimaryID: Determining the redundancy ID of the primary CPU (S7-1500)_

RH_GetPrimaryID: Determining the redundancy ID of the primary CPU
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Description
 The "RH_GetPrimaryID" instruction is used to determine the redundancy ID of the primary CPU.
 You can use this instruction in all system states even if the pairing of the two R or H CPUs has not yet been performed.
 
 Parameter
 The following table shows the parameters of the instruction "RH_GetPrimaryID":
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Parameter
 
 
 Declaration
 
 
 Data type
 
 
 Description
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ret_Val
 
 
 Output
 
 
 INT
 
 
 Return value
 
 
 
 
 
 
 You can find additional information on valid data types under: Overview of the valid data types
 
 Ret_Val parameter
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Error code* (W#16#...)
 
 
 Explanation
 
 
 
 
 
 
 1
 
 
 The CPU with redundancy ID 1 is the primary CPU.
 
 
 
 
 2
 
 
 The CPU with redundancy ID 2 is the primary CPU.
 
 
 
 
 8091
 
 
 No data available, for example because "RH_GetPrimaryID" is not supported by your CPU.
 
 
 
 
 * The error codes in the program editor are displayed as integer or hexadecimal values. For information on switching the display formats, refer to "See also".
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 See also
 
 
 Switching 


| Parameter | Declaration | Data type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ret_Val | Output | INT | Return value |


| Error code* (W#16#...) | Explanation |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | The CPU with redundancy ID 1 is the primary CPU. |
| 2 | The CPU with redundancy ID 2 is the primary CPU. |
| 8091 | No data available, for example because "RH_GetPrimaryID" is not supported by your CPU. |
| * The error codes in the program editor are displayed as integer or hexadecimal values. For information on switching the display formats, refer to "See also". |


### ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/142297473419.htm|ACK_FCT_WARN: Acknowledge warning message for exceeding the F-cycle time (S7-1200, S7-1500)

_ItemId: ProgExtInstr2MenUS/15889165323/142297473419.htm|ACK_FCT_WARN: Acknowledge warning message for exceeding the F-cycle time (S7-1200, S7-1500)_

ACK_FCT_WARN: Acknowledge warning message for exceeding the F-cycle time
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Description
 You can use the "ACK_FCT_WARN" instruction to acknowledge the message in the diagnostic buffer that warns you about exceeding the F-cycle time. The F-cycle time is the maximum time that may pass between two F-OB calls.
 If the instruction at input parameter "ACK_WARN" detects a rising edge, the warning message is acknowledged. In all other cases it is not acknowledged. 
 You can find more information on the F-cycle time in the "SIMATIC Safety - Configuring and Programming" Operating Manual.
 
 Parameters
 The following table shows the parameters of the "ACK_FCT_WARN" instruction:
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Parameter
 
 
 Declaration
 
 
 Data type
 
 
 Description
 
 
 
 
 
 
 ACK_WARN
 
 
 Input
 
 
 BOOL
 
 
 Acknowledgment of the warning message on rising edge
 
 
 
 
 
 
 You can find additional information on valid data types under: Overview of the valid data types


| Parameter | Declaration | Data type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ACK_WARN | Input | BOOL | Acknowledgment of the warning message on rising edge |
